request.description:
This endpoint allows you to update additional details of a car. The request should be sent as an HTTP PUT to {{baseUrl}}/host/carAdditional. The request body should be of form-data type and include the following parameters:
- carId (text): The ID of the car.
- carImage_1 (file): The image file of the car.
- carImage_2 (file): An additional image file of the car.
- ac (text): Description of the air conditioning system.
- musicSystem (text): Description of the music system in the car.
- autoWindow (text): Description of the automatic window system.
- sunRoof (text): Description of the sunroof feature.
- touchScreen (text): Description of the touchscreen system in the car.
- sevenSeater (text): Description of the seven-seater configuration.
- reverseCamera (text): Description of the reverse camera feature.
- transmission (text): Description of the transmission type.
- fuelType (text): Description of the fuel type used.
- airBags (text): Description of the airbag system in the car.
- horsePower (text): Description of the horsepower of the car.

The response to this request will be documented as a JSON schema.

request.description:
### Update Listing

This endpoint is used to update a listing with the provided details.

#### Request Body
- `listingId` (string): The ID of the listing to be updated.
- `carId` (string): The ID of the car associated with the listing.
- `details` (string): Additional details about the listing.
- `startDate` (string): The start date of the listing.
- `startTime` (string): The start time of the listing.
- `endDate` (string): The end date of the listing.
- `endTime` (string): The end time of the listing.
- `pauseTimeStartDate` (string): The start date of any pause time during the listing.
- `pauseTimeEndDate` (string): The end date of any pause time during the listing.
- `pauseTimeStartTime` (string): The start time of any pause time during the listing.
- `pauseTimeEndTime` (string): The end time of any pause time during the listing.
- `hourCount` (integer): The count of hours for the listing.

#### Response
The response will contain the updated details of the listing.

### Delete Host Listing

This endpoint is used to delete a specific host listing.

**Request Body**
- `listingId`: The ID of the listing to be deleted. 

**Response**
The response will not contain a message body. A successful deletion will return a status code of 200.

request.description:
# Host Login

This endpoint is used to authenticate a user by logging in with their phone number and password.

## Request Body

- `phone` (string, required): The phone number of the user.
- `password` (string, required): The password for the user's account.
    

## Response

The response of this request is a JSON object with the following properties:

- `token` (string): The authentication token for the logged-in user.
- `userId` (string): The unique identifier of the user.
    

### Example

``` json
{
    "token": "************",
    "userId": "************"
}

 ```

### Get Car Registration Details

This endpoint allows you to retrieve car registration details.

#### Request Body

- `RegID` (string, required): The registration ID of the car.
    

#### Response

The response for this request is a JSON schema representing the structure of the response data.

request.description:
### API Request Description

This endpoint is used to sign up a new user by providing their phone number, password, and role.

### API Response

The response of this request is a JSON schema, which includes the structure and data types of the response fields.

### Get User Cars

This endpoint retrieves a list of cars associated with the user.

#### Request

- Method: GET
- URL: `{{baseUrl}}/users/cars`
    

#### Response

The response will include an array of car objects, each containing details such as the car's make, model, year, and other relevant information.

Example response:

``` json
[
    {
        "make": "Toyota",
        "model": "Camry",
        "year": 2018
    },
    {
        "make": "Honda",
        "model": "Civic",
        "year": 2019
    }
]

 ```

### Get User Cars

This endpoint retrieves a list of cars associated with the user.

#### Request

- Method: GET
- URL: `{{baseUrl}}/users/cars`
    

#### Response

The response will include an array of car objects, each containing details such as the car's make, model, year, and other relevant information.

Example response:

``` json
[
    {
        "make": "Toyota",
        "model": "Camry",
        "year": 2018
    },
    {
        "make": "Honda",
        "model": "Civic",
        "year": 2019
    }
]

 ```

### Trip Started

This endpoint is used to indicate that a trip has started for a specific user.

#### Request Body
- bookingId (string, required): The ID of the booking for which the trip has started.

#### Response
The response for this request is a JSON schema. Please refer to the API documentation for the JSON schema definition.

### Trip Started

This endpoint is used to indicate that a trip has started for a specific user.

#### Request Body
- bookingId (string, required): The ID of the booking for which the trip has started.

#### Response
The response for this request is a JSON schema. Please refer to the API documentation for the JSON schema definition.

### Cancel Booking

This endpoint is used to cancel a booking for a user.

#### Request Body
- `bookingId` (string, required): The ID of the booking to be cancelled.

Example:
```json
{
    "bookingId": "your_booking_id"
}
```

#### Response
The response will include the status of the cancellation request.

The `POST /users/findcars` endpoint is used to find cars based on the specified start and end dates, as well as start and end times.

### Request Body

- `startDate` (string): The start date for the car search.
- `endDate` (string): The end date for the car search.
- `startTime` (string): The start time for the car search.
- `endTime` (string): The end time for the car search.
    

### Response

The response for this request is a JSON schema representing the structure of the response data.
